mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio for the combined signal falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative 5-day returns during expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening labor demand, which supports consumer spending forecasts and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently precedes negative equity returns, or the correlation coefficient between payroll changes and 20-day returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 250.0, accelerating inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPIAUCSL breaches of 250.0, or inflation spikes fail to trigger subsequent rate hikes and negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of high GDP growth and low rates coincides with equity drawdowns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 90.0 are followed by positive equity returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the threshold fails to predict short-term market direction.
